annotations.label: Differential equation

anchor.snippet: a differential equation is an equation that relates one or more unknown functions and their derivatives

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes ODEs as integral curves of (time-dependent) vector fields via IsIntegralCurve, but has no general notion of an arbitrary differential equation.

annotations.label: Bernoulli differential equation

anchor.snippet: This is an ordinary differential equation of the form

annotations.note: No Bernoulli equation or its solution method appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equation of motion

anchor.snippet: called an equation of motion

annotations.note: This physics example equation of motion is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Velocity of a falling ball

anchor.snippet: the determination of the velocity of a ball falling through the air

annotations.note: This applied physics example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ordinary differential equation (ODE)

anchor.snippet: An ordinary differential equation ( ODE ) is an equation containing an unknown function of one real or complex variable

annotations.note: IsIntegralCurve γ v formalizes solutions of first-order ODE systems y' = v(t,y), but Mathlib has no general ODE object covering arbitrary order/form.

annotations.label: Partial differential equation (PDE)

anchor.snippet: A partial differential equation ( PDE ) is a differential equation that contains unknown multivariable functions and their partial derivatives

annotations.note: Mathlib has only specific PDE instances (e.g. the Laplacian/harmonic functions), not a general PDE notion.

annotations.label: Linear differential equation

anchor.snippet: Linear differential equations are differential equations that are linear in the unknown function and its derivatives

annotations.note: No general notion of a linear differential equation exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-linear differential equation

anchor.snippet: A non-linear differential equation is a differential equation that is not a linear equation in the unknown function and its derivatives

annotations.note: No notion of nonlinear differential equation is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Order of a differential equation

anchor.snippet: The order of the differential equation is the highest order of derivative of the unknown function that appears in the differential equation

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general differential-equation object and hence no definition of its order.

annotations.label: Degree of a differential equation

anchor.snippet: the degree of the differential equation is, depending on the context, the polynomial degree in the highest derivative of the unknown function

annotations.note: The degree of a differential equation is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homogeneous linear equation

anchor.snippet: A linear differential equation is homogeneous if each term in the equation includes either the dependent variable or one of its derivatives

annotations.note: No notion of a homogeneous linear differential equation is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inhomogeneous first-order linear ODE

anchor.snippet: Inhomogeneous first-order linear constant-coefficient ordinary differential equation

annotations.note: This specific example equation and its solution are not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homogeneous second-order linear ODE

anchor.snippet: Homogeneous second-order linear ordinary differential equation

annotations.note: This example second-order linear ODE is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Harmonic oscillator equation

anchor.snippet: describing the harmonic oscillator

annotations.note: Mathlib proves derivatives of sin/cos (hasDerivAt_sin/cos) but has no statement that they solve the harmonic-oscillator equation y''+ω²y=0.

annotations.label: First-order nonlinear ODE

anchor.snippet: First-order nonlinear ordinary differential equation

annotations.note: This nonlinear example ODE is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pendulum equation

anchor.snippet: describing the motion of a pendulum of length L

annotations.note: The pendulum equation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homogeneous first-order linear PDE

anchor.snippet: Homogeneous first-order linear partial differential equation

annotations.note: This example first-order linear PDE is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Laplace equation

anchor.snippet: of elliptic type, the Laplace equation

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Laplacian Δ (InnerProductSpace.laplacian) and HarmonicAt f x as Δf =ᶠ 0, i.e. exactly the solutions of the Laplace equation.

annotations.label: KdV equation

anchor.snippet: Third-order non-linear partial differential equation, the KdV equation

annotations.note: The Korteweg–de Vries equation is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General solution contains n constants

anchor.snippet: The general solution of a first-order ordinary differential equation includes a constant

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theorem that the general solution of an nth-order ODE depends on n constants (only the discrete analog for LinearRecurrence).

annotations.label: Initial value problem

anchor.snippet: form what is known as an initial value problem

annotations.note: Mathlib has no explicit IVP definition, but the Picard–Lindelöf existence theorems produce solutions satisfying a prescribed initial condition α t₀ = x.

annotations.label: Boundary value problem

anchor.snippet: boundary conditions lead to a boundary value problem

annotations.note: Boundary value problems are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Peano existence theorem

anchor.snippet: the Peano existence theorem gives one set of circumstances in which a solution exists

annotations.note: Mathlib has the Picard–Lindelöf existence theorem (requiring Lipschitz continuity) but not Peano's theorem, which guarantees existence under mere continuity.

annotations.label: Existence and uniqueness for nth-order linear IVP

anchor.snippet: are continuous on some interval containing

annotations.note: Mathlib gives existence (Picard–Lindelöf) and uniqueness (ODE_solution_unique) for first-order ODE systems under Lipschitz hypotheses, but not the specific nth-order linear continuity result.

annotations.label: Delay differential equation (DDE)

anchor.snippet: A delay differential equation (DDE) is an equation for a function of a single variable

annotations.note: Delay differential equations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Integral equation

anchor.snippet: Integral equations may be viewed as the analog to differential equations

annotations.note: No notion of integral equation (Volterra/Fredholm) is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Integro-differential equation (IDE)

anchor.snippet: An integro-differential equation (IDE) is an equation that combines aspects of a differential equation and an integral equation

annotations.note: Integro-differential equations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stochastic differential equation (SDE)

anchor.snippet: A stochastic differential equation (SDE) is an equation in which the unknown quantity is a stochastic process

annotations.note: Stochastic differential equations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stochastic partial differential equation (SPDE)

anchor.snippet: A stochastic partial differential equation (SPDE) is an equation that generalizes SDEs to include space-time noise processes

annotations.note: Stochastic partial differential equations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ultrametric pseudo-differential equation

anchor.snippet: An ultrametric pseudo-differential equation is an equation which contains p-adic numbers in an ultrametric space

annotations.note: Ultrametric/p-adic pseudo-differential equations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Differential algebraic equation (DAE)

anchor.snippet: A differential algebraic equation (DAE) is a differential equation comprising differential and algebraic terms

annotations.note: Differential algebraic equations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Difference equation

anchor.snippet: the independent variable assumes only discrete values

annotations.note: LinearRecurrence with IsSolution formalizes linear homogeneous constant-coefficient difference equations, a special case of general difference equations.
